meta.name: Banku Pepper Sauce
meta.subtitle: Fresh Ghana-style pepper sauce for banku, kenkey, fish, and grilled meats

source.name: ssa
notes: Fresh, raw pepper sauce commonly served with banku/kenkey and fish.
Designed to be heat-scalable and easy to make without a blender if needed.

summary: A bright, fiery, fresh pepper-onion-tomato condiment used to lift banku, kenkey, grilled fish, and soups.

yield.notes: Makes about 1 to 1 1/4 cups (10–12 condiment servings).

ingredients.label: Scotch bonnet or habanero peppers
notes: Use 1–2 for mild/medium, 3–4 for hot, 5+ for very hot.
Remove seeds for slightly less heat (still hot).

ingredients.label: Tomato
ingredients.notes: Optional but common; adds moisture and balances heat.

ingredients.label: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Optional; adds a sharper savory edge.

ingredients.label: Lime juice
ingredients.notes: Fresh is best. Lemon juice also works.

ingredients.label: Neutral oil (optional)
ingredients.notes: Optional—rounds the bite slightly. Many versions use no oil.

ingredients.label: Smoked fish powder or crushed smoked fish (optional)
ingredients.notes: Optional, non-vegan boost commonly used alongside fish dishes.

steps.title: Prep and choose your method
instructions: Wash peppers and tomato. Peel onion and garlic (if using).
• Mortar & pestle (most traditional texture): crush peppers + salt first, then onion, then tomato.
• Blender (fast, smoother): pulse everything in short bursts to keep some texture.
• Knife-chop (no tools): mince peppers and onion very finely; chop tomato small and mix.
checks: Ingredients are ready and your method is selected.

steps.title: Crush/pulse the peppers and aromatics
instructions: Mortar & pestle: add peppers + salt and crush into a coarse paste. Add onion and crush again.
If using garlic/ginger, add and crush until fragrant.
Blender: add peppers, onion, tomato, salt, lime juice, and optional garlic/ginger. Pulse 3–6 times.
Aim for a coarse sauce with visible pepper/onion bits (not a perfectly smooth puree).
checks: Sauce is coarse and aromatic; not watery.

steps.title: Finish and adjust
instructions: Stir in lime juice (if not already added) and optional oil.
Taste: add a pinch more salt if needed. Add more lime for brightness.
Optional umami: stir in smoked fish powder/crushed smoked fish if serving with banku + fish.
Rest 5 minutes for flavors to mingle (optional but recommended).
checks: Heat/salt/acid feel balanced for a condiment.

pairings.notes: Serve alongside banku/kenkey as a dip or spooned over grilled fish.

fridge.notes: Store covered. Fresh/raw sauces mellow over time; heat remains strong.

reheat.notes: Serve cold or at room temperature.

notes: Handle hot peppers with gloves if sensitive; avoid touching eyes.
For a thicker sauce: use less tomato or drain watery tomato seeds before adding.
For a sharper onion bite: rinse chopped onion briefly and drain before mixing.

adapt.notes: Use 1–2 peppers and add extra tomato; remove seeds and membranes.
Use 5–6 peppers; skip tomato or use half. Add a pinch more salt and lime to keep balance.
Omit tomato for a drier, sharper sauce—great for grilled meats and fish.
